examples.en: We were about to order when the waiter brought the wrong menu.
examples.zh: 我們正要點餐時，服務生拿錯了菜單。

gept.pattern: Tense: keep / keep on + V-ing (持續反覆,keep 接動名詞)
gept.meaning_zh: keep / keep on 加動名詞表動作的持續或反覆發生。
gept.structure: S + keep (on) + V-ing

examples.en: He keeps forgetting where he put his keys.
examples.zh: 他老是忘記鑰匙放在哪裡。

examples.en: The baby kept crying throughout the night.
examples.zh: 那寶寶整晚一直哭。

examples.en: She keeps on asking me the same question.
examples.zh: 她一直反覆問我同一個問題。

gept.pattern: Tense: be used to vs get used to + V-ing (習慣於,狀態與過程)
gept.meaning_zh: be used to + V-ing 表「已習慣於」，get used to + V-ing 表「逐漸習慣」，與表過去的 used to 不同。
gept.structure: S + be / get used to + V-ing / N (習慣於某事)

examples.en: I'm used to waking up early for work.
examples.zh: 我已經習慣早起上班了。

examples.en: She is getting used to living in a big city.
examples.zh: 她正逐漸習慣住在大城市。

examples.en: It took him a while to get used to driving on the left.
examples.zh: 他花了好一陣子才習慣靠左行駛。

gept.pattern: Modal: ought to have + p.p. (助動詞,過去本該做卻沒做)
gept.meaning_zh: ought to have + p.p. 與 should have + p.p. 同義，表過去本該做卻沒做。
gept.structure: S + ought to have + V-p.p. (過去本該)

examples.en: You ought to have told me about the change of plans.
examples.zh: 你本該告訴我計畫變了。

examples.en: They ought to have booked a table in advance.
examples.zh: 他們本該事先訂位的。

examples.en: I ought to have checked the weather before leaving.
examples.zh: 我出門前本該查一下天氣的。

gept.pattern: Modal: will / would for willingness & refusal (助動詞,意願與拒絕)
gept.meaning_zh: will 表現在的意願，won't 表拒絕；過去用 would / wouldn't。
gept.structure: S + will / won't + V-原形 (意願 / 拒絕)

examples.en: I'll carry that bag for you if it's too heavy.
examples.zh: 如果那袋子太重，我幫你提。

examples.en: The old car won't start in cold weather.
examples.zh: 這台老車天冷時就是發不動。

examples.en: He wouldn't listen to anyone's advice back then.
examples.zh: 他那時候就是不肯聽任何人的建議。

gept.pattern: Modal: should / shall in formal rules (助動詞,規章中的 shall / should)
gept.meaning_zh: 在規章、合約或正式說明中，shall / should 用來表規定或義務。
gept.structure: S + shall / should + V-原形 (規定義務)

examples.en: Visitors shall register at the front desk upon arrival.
examples.zh: 訪客抵達時應在櫃台登記。

examples.en: All staff should wear identification at all times.
examples.zh: 全體員工應隨時配戴識別證。

examples.en: Passengers shall not lean against the doors.
examples.zh: 乘客不得倚靠車門。

gept.pattern: Modal: must vs have to: obligation source (助動詞,內在義務與外在規定)
gept.meaning_zh: must 多表說話者自身的主觀要求，have to 多表外部規定或客觀必要。
gept.structure: S + must + V-原形 (主觀) vs S + have to + V-原形 (外部)

examples.en: I must remember to call my grandmother tonight.
examples.zh: 我一定要記得今晚打給奶奶（自己的要求）。

examples.en: You have to wear a helmet when riding a motorbike here.
examples.zh: 在這裡騎機車必須戴安全帽（規定）。

examples.en: We have to submit the form before Friday.
examples.zh: 我們必須在星期五前繳交表格。

gept.pattern: Subjunctive: were to (假設語氣,were to 表純粹假想)
gept.meaning_zh: if S were to + V 用於假設一個純屬想像或可能性較低的未來情況。
gept.structure: If + S + were to + V-原形, S + would + V-原形

examples.en: If you were to win the lottery, what would you do first?
examples.zh: 假如你中了樂透，你會先做什麼？

examples.en: If the company were to close, hundreds would lose their jobs.
examples.zh: 萬一公司倒閉，數百人會失業。

examples.en: If we were to move, I'd miss this neighborhood.
examples.zh: 假如我們搬家，我會很想念這個社區。

gept.pattern: Inversion: not until (倒裝句,Not until 置句首倒裝)
gept.meaning_zh: Not until 置於句首時，主要子句須部分倒裝。
gept.structure: Not until + 時間/子句 + 助動詞 + S + V

examples.en: Not until midnight did the guests finally leave.
examples.zh: 直到午夜客人才終於離開。

examples.en: Not until she apologized did he calm down.
examples.zh: 直到她道歉，他才冷靜下來。

examples.en: Not until I got home did I realize I'd lost my wallet.
examples.zh: 直到回到家我才發現錢包弄丟了。

gept.pattern: Inversion: not only ... but also (倒裝句,Not only 置句首倒裝)
gept.meaning_zh: Not only 置於句首時，其後子句須部分倒裝。
gept.structure: Not only + 助動詞 + S + V, but + S + also + V

examples.en: Not only did she sing, but she also played the piano.
examples.zh: 她不僅唱了歌，還彈了鋼琴。

examples.en: Not only is he talented, but he is also very humble.
examples.zh: 他不僅有才華，而且非常謙虛。

examples.en: Not only did they arrive late, but they also forgot the tickets.
examples.zh: 他們不僅遲到，還忘了帶票。

gept.pattern: Inversion: so / such ... that (倒裝句,So / Such 置句首倒裝)
gept.meaning_zh: So + 形容詞 / Such + 名詞置於句首表強調時，須部分倒裝。
gept.structure: So + adj + 助動詞 + S + that... / Such + N + 助動詞 + S + that...

examples.en: So loud was the music that we couldn't hear each other.
examples.zh: 音樂大聲到我們彼此都聽不見。

examples.en: Such was his fame that everyone recognized him.
examples.zh: 他名氣大到人人都認得他。

examples.en: So tired were the children that they fell asleep in the car.
examples.zh: 孩子們累到在車上就睡著了。

gept.pattern: Inversion: little / never / rarely (倒裝句,否定 / 半否定副詞置句首)
gept.meaning_zh: little、never、rarely、seldom 等否定或半否定副詞置句首時須倒裝。
gept.structure: Little / Never / Rarely + 助動詞 + S + V

examples.en: Little did I know that my life was about to change.
examples.zh: 我當時完全不知道我的人生即將改變。

examples.en: Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.
examples.zh: 我從沒見過這麼美的日落。

examples.en: Rarely does he miss a day of work.
examples.zh: 他很少請假不上班。

gept.pattern: Emphasis: it was ... that cleft for time / place (分裂句,強調時間或地點)
gept.meaning_zh: It was + 時間/地點 + that 用來強調句中的時間或地點成分。
gept.structure: It + be + 時間/地點 + that + S + V

examples.en: It was last summer that we first met.
examples.zh: 我們是去年夏天第一次見面的。

examples.en: It was at the airport that she lost her passport.
examples.zh: 她就是在機場弄丟護照的。

examples.en: It was in 2010 that the company was founded.
examples.zh: 這家公司是在二○一○年創立的。

gept.pattern: Noun clause as subject (名詞子句當主詞)
gept.meaning_zh: 由 that 引導的名詞子句可當句子主詞,但口語常以 it 作虛主詞、把子句後移。
gept.structure: That + S + V + ... + V (子句當主詞); 常改寫為 It + V + that + S + V

examples.en: That she passed the exam surprised everyone.
examples.zh: 她通過考試這件事讓大家很驚訝。

examples.en: It is clear that he forgot our meeting.
examples.zh: 很明顯他忘了我們的會議。

examples.en: That we won the game made us proud.
examples.zh: 我們贏了比賽讓我們很自豪。

gept.pattern: Noun clause as object (名詞子句當受詞)
gept.meaning_zh: that 引導的名詞子句當動詞受詞時,that 在口語中常可省略。
gept.structure: S + V(think/know/believe…) + (that) + S + V

examples.en: I think (that) it will rain tonight.
examples.zh: 我認為今晚會下雨。

examples.en: She knows you are right.
examples.zh: 她知道你是對的。

examples.en: We hope that everything goes well.
examples.zh: 我們希望一切順利。

gept.pattern: Noun clause as complement (名詞子句當主詞補語)
gept.meaning_zh: that 引導的名詞子句可放在 be 動詞後當主詞補語,說明主詞內容。
gept.structure: S + be + that + S + V (補語)

examples.en: The problem is that we have no money left.
examples.zh: 問題在於我們沒錢了。

examples.en: My worry is that she might be late.
examples.zh: 我擔心的是她可能會遲到。

examples.en: The truth is that nobody knew the answer.
examples.zh: 事實是沒人知道答案。

gept.pattern: Wh- noun clause (wh- 名詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: what/who/where/how 等引導的名詞子句可當主詞或受詞,子句內用直述語序。
gept.structure: S + V + what/who/where/how + S + V (直述語序,非問句語序)

examples.en: I don't know what he wants.
examples.zh: 我不知道他要什麼。

examples.en: What you said really helped me.
examples.zh: 你說的話真的幫了我。

examples.en: Tell me where you put the keys.
examples.zh: 告訴我你把鑰匙放哪了。

gept.pattern: Whether ... or (whether 名詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: whether 引導名詞子句表「是否」,可加 or not;比 if 更正式且可當主詞。
gept.structure: whether (or not) + S + V; S + V + whether + S + V (or not)

examples.en: Whether we go or not depends on the weather.
examples.zh: 我們去不去要看天氣。

examples.en: I'm not sure whether she likes coffee.
examples.zh: 我不確定她是否喜歡咖啡。

examples.en: Let me know whether you can come.
examples.zh: 讓我知道你能不能來。

gept.pattern: Adverbial clause of time: while / as / as soon as (時間副詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: 以 while、as、as soon as 等連接詞引導,說明主要動作發生的時間。
gept.structure: S + V, as soon as / while / as + S + V (時間連接詞引導)

examples.en: As soon as I got home, I called you.
examples.zh: 我一到家就打給你了。

examples.en: She sang while she was cooking.
examples.zh: 她邊煮飯邊唱歌。

examples.en: As I grew older, I understood my parents more.
examples.zh: 隨著年紀漸長,我更懂父母了。

gept.pattern: Adverbial clause of reason: as / now that (原因副詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: 以 as、now that、seeing that 引導表原因,now that 強調「既然(現況)」。
gept.structure: As / Now that / Seeing that + S + V, S + V

examples.en: Now that you're here, we can start.
examples.zh: 既然你來了,我們可以開始了。

examples.en: As it was raining, we stayed inside.
examples.zh: 因為在下雨,我們待在室內。

examples.en: Seeing that he was tired, she drove instead.
examples.zh: 看到他累了,她改由她開車。

gept.pattern: Adverbial clause of purpose: so that / in order that (目的副詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: so that、in order that 引導目的子句,子句內常用 can/could/will/would。
gept.structure: S + V + so that / in order that + S + can/could/will/would + V

examples.en: I left early so that I could catch the train.
examples.zh: 我早點離開好趕上火車。

examples.en: She speaks slowly so that everyone can understand.
examples.zh: 她說得很慢好讓每個人都聽懂。

examples.en: We saved money in order that we could travel.
examples.zh: 我們存錢以便能去旅行。

gept.pattern: Adverbial clause of result: so...that / such...that (結果副詞子句)
gept.meaning_zh: so + 形容詞/副詞 + that、such + (a) + 名詞 + that,表「如此…以致於…」。
gept.structure: so + adj/adv + that + S + V; such + (a/an) + (adj) + N + that + S + V

examples.en: It was so cold that the lake froze.
examples.zh: 天氣冷到湖都結冰了。

examples.en: He is such a kind man that everyone likes him.
examples.zh: 他是個如此善良的人,大家都喜歡他。

examples.en: She ran so fast that no one could catch her.
examples.zh: 她跑得太快,沒人追得上。

gept.pattern: so + adj vs such + N (so 與 such 的差異)
gept.meaning_zh: so 後接形容詞或副詞;such 後接(冠詞+)名詞片語。兩者皆表強調「如此」。
gept.structure: so + adj/adv; such + (a/an) + (adj +) N

examples.en: The movie was so boring.
examples.zh: 那部電影真無聊。

examples.en: It was such a boring movie.
examples.zh: 那真是部無聊的電影。

examples.en: You are so kind to help me.
examples.zh: 你真好心幫我。

gept.pattern: Adverbial clause of concession: while / whereas (讓步副詞子句,對比)
gept.meaning_zh: while、whereas 引導對比子句,表「然而、反之」,強調前後事實相反。
gept.structure: While / Whereas + S + V, S + V (前後對比)

examples.en: While I like tea, my sister prefers coffee.
examples.zh: 我喜歡茶,而我妹妹偏好咖啡。

examples.en: He is quiet, whereas his brother is talkative.
examples.zh: 他很安靜,反觀他哥哥很多話。

examples.en: Some people save money, while others spend it all.
examples.zh: 有些人存錢,有些人則全花光。

gept.pattern: No matter + wh- (無論…讓步子句)
gept.meaning_zh: no matter how/what/who/where 引導讓步子句,表「無論…都」,等於 however 等。
gept.structure: No matter + how/what/who/where + S + V, S + V

examples.en: No matter how hard I try, I can't fix it.
examples.zh: 無論我多努力都修不好。

examples.en: No matter what you say, I won't change my mind.
examples.zh: 不管你說什麼,我都不會改變心意。

examples.en: No matter where you go, I'll find you.
examples.zh: 無論你去哪,我都會找到你。

gept.pattern: -ever clause: whatever / whoever / whenever (-ever 子句)
gept.meaning_zh: whatever、whoever、whenever 等表「任何…/無論…」,可當名詞子句或讓步子句。
gept.structure: whatever / whoever / whenever / wherever + S + V

examples.en: You can order whatever you like.
examples.zh: 你想點什麼都可以。

examples.en: Whoever finishes first wins a prize.
examples.zh: 誰先完成就得獎。

examples.en: Call me whenever you need help.
examples.zh: 你需要幫忙時隨時打給我。

gept.pattern: as if / as though (好像…一樣)
gept.meaning_zh: as if、as though 引導表「好像」;用過去式表與事實相反的假想。
gept.structure: S + V + as if / as though + S + (過去式,表與現實相反)

examples.en: He talks as if he knew everything.
examples.zh: 他講得好像什麼都懂似的。

examples.en: She looks as though she has seen a ghost.
examples.zh: 她看起來好像見了鬼。

examples.en: They acted as if nothing had happened.
examples.zh: 他們表現得好像什麼都沒發生過。

gept.pattern: in case (以防萬一)
gept.meaning_zh: in case 引導預防性子句,表「以防、萬一」;子句多用現在式,不用 will。
gept.structure: S + V + in case + S + V (現在式,表預防)

examples.en: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
examples.zh: 帶把傘以防下雨。

examples.en: I'll write it down in case I forget.
examples.zh: 我把它寫下來免得忘記。

examples.en: Save your work in case the computer crashes.
examples.zh: 存檔以防電腦當機。

gept.pattern: Participial construction - active (分詞構句,主動 V-ing)
gept.meaning_zh: 當兩句主詞相同,可把表時間/原因的副詞子句簡化為現在分詞片語(主動)。
gept.structure: V-ing + ..., S + V (主詞與主句相同,主動關係)

examples.en: Hearing the news, she burst into tears.
examples.zh: 聽到那消息,她放聲大哭。

examples.en: Walking down the street, I met an old friend.
examples.zh: 走在街上時,我遇到一位老朋友。

examples.en: Feeling tired, he went to bed early.
examples.zh: 因為覺得累,他早早就睡了。

gept.pattern: Participial construction - passive (分詞構句,被動 V-p.p.)
gept.meaning_zh: 當主詞與動作為被動關係時,分詞構句用過去分詞(p.p.)開頭。
gept.structure: V-p.p. + ..., S + V (被動關係,可由 Being p.p. 省略 Being)

examples.en: Written in simple English, the book is easy to read.
examples.zh: 由於用簡單英文寫成,這本書很好讀。

examples.en: Shocked by the result, they said nothing.
examples.zh: 被結果震驚,他們什麼都沒說。

examples.en: Built in 1920, the house still stands today.
examples.zh: 建於 1920 年,這棟房子今天仍屹立。

gept.pattern: Perfect participle: having + p.p. (完成分詞)
gept.meaning_zh: having + p.p. 強調分詞動作先於主句動作完成。
gept.structure: Having + p.p. + ..., S + V (分詞動作較早完成)

examples.en: Having finished his homework, he went out to play.
examples.zh: 做完作業後,他出去玩了。

examples.en: Having lived there for years, she knows the city well.
examples.zh: 在那住了多年,她對這城市很熟。

examples.en: Having lost the keys, we couldn't get in.
examples.zh: 因為弄丟了鑰匙,我們進不去。

gept.pattern: With + O + participle (with 附帶狀況)
gept.meaning_zh: with + 受詞 + 分詞/形容詞/介系詞片語,表伴隨的狀態或附帶情況。
gept.structure: with + O + V-ing / V-p.p. / adj / 介系詞片語 (附帶狀況)

examples.en: She fell asleep with the TV on.
examples.zh: 她開著電視睡著了。

examples.en: He stood there with his arms folded.
examples.zh: 他抱著手臂站在那。

examples.en: They left with the door open.
examples.zh: 他們離開時門開著。

gept.pattern: Gerund after preposition phrase (固定片語後接動名詞)
gept.meaning_zh: look forward to、be used to、object to 等以 to 結尾的片語,to 為介系詞,後接動名詞。
gept.structure: look forward to / be used to / object to + V-ing

examples.en: I look forward to hearing from you.
examples.zh: 我期待收到你的回覆。

examples.en: She is used to getting up early.
examples.zh: 她習慣早起。

examples.en: They object to working on weekends.
examples.zh: 他們反對週末上班。

gept.pattern: It's no use / no good + V-ing (做…沒用)
gept.meaning_zh: It's no use / no good + 動名詞,表「做某事沒有用」。
gept.structure: It's no use / no good + V-ing

examples.en: It's no use crying over spilt milk.
examples.zh: 覆水難收,哭也沒用。

examples.en: It's no good arguing with him.
examples.zh: 跟他爭也沒用。

examples.en: It was no use waiting any longer.
examples.zh: 再等下去也沒用。

gept.pattern: be worth + V-ing (值得做)
gept.meaning_zh: be worth 後接動名詞,表「值得做某事」,動名詞為主動形但含被動意義。
gept.structure: S + be worth + V-ing

examples.en: This museum is worth visiting.
examples.zh: 這座博物館值得參觀。

examples.en: The movie is worth watching twice.
examples.zh: 這部電影值得看兩遍。

examples.en: Is it worth trying again?
examples.zh: 再試一次值得嗎?

gept.pattern: have difficulty / trouble in + V-ing (做…有困難)
gept.meaning_zh: have difficulty / trouble + (in) + 動名詞,表「做某事有困難」,in 常省略。
gept.structure: have difficulty / trouble + (in) + V-ing

examples.en: I have difficulty understanding his accent.
examples.zh: 我聽懂他的口音有困難。

examples.en: She had trouble finding a parking space.
examples.zh: 她找停車位很費力。

examples.en: We had no difficulty solving the puzzle.
examples.zh: 我們解這謎題毫不費力。

gept.pattern: spend / waste + time / money + V-ing (花時間 / 錢做…)
gept.meaning_zh: spend / waste + 時間或金錢 + 動名詞,表「花/浪費…做某事」。
gept.structure: S + spend / waste + 時間/金錢 + (on) + V-ing

examples.en: He spends hours playing video games.
examples.zh: 他花好幾個鐘頭打電動。

examples.en: Don't waste money buying things you don't need.
examples.zh: 別浪費錢買用不到的東西。

examples.en: She spent the whole weekend cleaning the house.
examples.zh: 她整個週末都在打掃房子。

gept.pattern: can't help + V-ing (忍不住做…)
gept.meaning_zh: can't help + 動名詞,表「忍不住做某事、不得不」。
gept.structure: S + can't help + V-ing

examples.en: I can't help laughing at his jokes.
examples.zh: 我忍不住對他的笑話發笑。

examples.en: She couldn't help crying at the ending.
examples.zh: 看到結局她忍不住哭了。

examples.en: We can't help worrying about you.
examples.zh: 我們忍不住為你擔心。

gept.pattern: feel like + V-ing (想要做…)
gept.meaning_zh: feel like + 動名詞,表「想要做某事」。
gept.structure: S + feel like + V-ing

examples.en: I feel like taking a walk.
examples.zh: 我想去散個步。

examples.en: Do you feel like eating out tonight?
examples.zh: 今晚你想出去吃嗎?

examples.en: She didn't feel like talking.
examples.zh: 她不想說話。

gept.pattern: Possessive + gerund (所有格 + 動名詞)
gept.meaning_zh: 動名詞的邏輯主詞用所有格(或受格)表示,說明是「誰做這動作」。
gept.structure: 所有格 (my/his/Tom's) + V-ing

examples.en: I appreciate your helping me.
examples.zh: 我很感謝你幫我。

examples.en: Do you mind my opening the window?
examples.zh: 你介意我開窗嗎?

examples.en: His being late annoyed everyone.
examples.zh: 他遲到惹惱了大家。

gept.pattern: It + adj + for / of + sb + to V (虛主詞 it 接不定詞)
gept.meaning_zh: it 當虛主詞,真主詞為不定詞;客觀難易用 for sb,品格評價用 of sb。
gept.structure: It + be + adj + for sb + to V (客觀); It + be + adj + of sb + to V (品格)

examples.en: It is hard for me to wake up early.
examples.zh: 早起對我來說很難。

examples.en: It was kind of you to help.
examples.zh: 你願意幫忙真好心。

examples.en: It is important for students to read every day.
examples.zh: 學生每天閱讀很重要。

gept.pattern: It takes + sb + time + to V (花…時間做…)
gept.meaning_zh: 以 it 當虛主詞,表「(某人)花多少時間做某事」。
gept.structure: It takes + (sb) + 時間 + to V

examples.en: It takes ten minutes to walk there.
examples.zh: 走到那要十分鐘。

examples.en: It took me a year to learn to drive.
examples.zh: 我花了一年學會開車。

examples.en: How long does it take to cook rice?
examples.zh: 煮飯要花多久?

gept.pattern: Bare infinitive after verbs of perception (感官動詞 + 原形 / V-ing)
gept.meaning_zh: see/hear/watch/feel 等感官動詞 + 受詞 + 原形動詞(看全程)或 V-ing(看片段、進行中)。
gept.structure: see/hear/watch/feel + O + V(原形) / V-ing

examples.en: I saw him cross the street.
examples.zh: 我看見他穿越馬路(全程)。

examples.en: I saw him crossing the street.
examples.zh: 我看見他正在穿越馬路。

examples.en: We heard someone shouting outside.
examples.zh: 我們聽見有人在外面喊叫。

gept.pattern: prefer A to B / prefer to V rather than V (偏好 A 勝於 B)
gept.meaning_zh: prefer 表偏好;比較名詞/動名詞用 prefer A to B,比較不定詞用 prefer to V rather than V。
gept.structure: prefer + N/V-ing + to + N/V-ing; prefer + to V + rather than + V(原形)

examples.en: I prefer tea to coffee.
examples.zh: 比起咖啡我更喜歡茶。

examples.en: She prefers walking to driving.
examples.zh: 她偏好走路勝過開車。

examples.en: He prefers to stay home rather than go out.
examples.zh: 他寧願待在家也不出門。

gept.pattern: would rather + V: rather than (寧願做…)
gept.meaning_zh: would rather + 原形動詞,表「寧願」;比較對象用 rather than + 原形。
gept.structure: S + would rather + V(原形) + (rather than + V原形)

examples.en: I would rather stay in tonight.
examples.zh: 今晚我寧願待在家。

examples.en: She'd rather walk than wait for the bus.
examples.zh: 她寧願走路也不等公車。

examples.en: We would rather not talk about it.
examples.zh: 我們寧可不談這件事。

gept.pattern: not as / so ... as (不如…那麼)
gept.meaning_zh: not as/so + 形容詞/副詞 + as,表「不如…那麼」的同等否定比較。
gept.structure: not as / so + adj/adv + as + 比較對象

examples.en: This book is not as interesting as that one.
examples.zh: 這本書不如那本有趣。

examples.en: He doesn't run as fast as his brother.
examples.zh: 他跑得不如他弟弟快。

examples.en: Today is not so hot as yesterday.
examples.zh: 今天不如昨天熱。

gept.pattern: by far / much + 比較級 / 最高級 (大幅程度修飾)
gept.meaning_zh: by far、much、a lot、even 等可修飾比較級或最高級,加強程度。
gept.structure: much / a lot / even + 比較級; by far + the + 最高級

examples.en: This is by far the best meal I've had.
examples.zh: 這是我吃過最好的一餐,遠遠勝出。

examples.en: He is much taller than his sister.
examples.zh: 他比他妹妹高很多。

examples.en: Today is even colder than yesterday.
examples.zh: 今天比昨天還更冷。

gept.pattern: one of the + 最高級 + 複數名詞 (最…之一)
gept.meaning_zh: one of the + 最高級 + 複數名詞,表「最…的…之一」,名詞用複數。
gept.structure: one of the + 最高級 + 複數N

examples.en: She is one of the smartest students in class.
examples.zh: 她是班上最聰明的學生之一。

examples.en: It's one of the tallest buildings in the city.
examples.zh: 它是市區最高的建築之一。

examples.en: This is one of the best movies of the year.
examples.zh: 這是今年最棒的電影之一。

gept.pattern: so + aux + S / neither + aux + S (附和,也…)
gept.meaning_zh: 表「…也是」:肯定用 so + 助動詞 + 主詞,否定用 neither/nor + 助動詞 + 主詞,且主詞動詞倒裝。
gept.structure: So + 助動詞 + S (肯定附和); Neither/Nor + 助動詞 + S (否定附和)

examples.en: I love pizza. — So do I.
examples.zh: 我愛披薩。──我也是。

examples.en: She can't swim. — Neither can I.
examples.zh: 她不會游泳。──我也不會。

examples.en: He has finished. — So have we.
examples.zh: 他做完了。──我們也是。

gept.pattern: Emphatic reflexive (反身代名詞表強調)
gept.meaning_zh: 反身代名詞(myself/himself…)可置於名詞後或句尾,強調「親自、本人」。
gept.structure: S + V + ... + 反身代名詞 (強調親自); 名詞 + 反身代名詞

examples.en: I made this cake myself.
examples.zh: 這蛋糕是我自己做的。

examples.en: The president himself attended the meeting.
examples.zh: 總統親自出席了會議。

examples.en: She fixed the car herself.
examples.zh: 她自己修好了車。

gept.pattern: all / both / half + of + the (整體數量詞 + of)
gept.meaning_zh: all、both、half、most 等修飾特定名詞時用「+ of + the/所有格 + 名詞」。
gept.structure: all / both / half / most + of + the/所有格 + N

examples.en: All of the students passed the test.
examples.zh: 所有學生都通過了測驗。

examples.en: Both of my parents are teachers.
examples.zh: 我父母都是老師。

examples.en: Half of the cake is gone.
examples.zh: 蛋糕被吃掉一半了。

gept.pattern: each / every (每一個的差異)
gept.meaning_zh: each、every 後接單數名詞與單數動詞;each 強調個別,every 強調整體全部。
gept.structure: each / every + 單數N + 單數V

examples.en: Each student has a locker.
examples.zh: 每個學生都有一個置物櫃。

examples.en: Every room in the hotel has a view.
examples.zh: 飯店每個房間都有景觀。

examples.en: Each of us was given a gift.
examples.zh: 我們每個人都拿到一份禮物。

gept.pattern: much / many / a lot of (可數與不可數的量詞)
gept.meaning_zh: many + 可數複數;much + 不可數;a lot of / lots of 兩者皆可,肯定句常用。
gept.structure: many + 可數複數N; much + 不可數N; a lot of + 可數/不可數N

examples.en: There aren't many people here.
examples.zh: 這裡人不多。

examples.en: We don't have much time.
examples.zh: 我們時間不多。

examples.en: She has a lot of friends.
examples.zh: 她有很多朋友。

gept.pattern: a number of vs the number of (數量 vs 數目)
gept.meaning_zh: a number of + 複數名詞,意為「許多」,動詞用複數;the number of 意為「…的數目」,動詞用單數。
gept.structure: A number of + 複數N + 複數V; The number of + 複數N + 單數V

examples.en: A number of students are absent today.
examples.zh: 今天有不少學生缺席。

examples.en: The number of students is increasing.
examples.zh: 學生人數正在增加。

examples.en: A number of cars were parked outside.
examples.zh: 外面停了不少車。

gept.pattern: too much / too many / too little / too few (過多過少)
gept.meaning_zh: too much/little 修飾不可數,too many/few 修飾可數複數,表數量過多或過少。
gept.structure: too much / too little + 不可數N; too many / too few + 可數複數N

examples.en: You put too much salt in the soup.
examples.zh: 你湯裡放太多鹽了。

examples.en: There are too many cars on the road.
examples.zh: 路上車子太多了。

examples.en: We have too little time and too few people.
examples.zh: 我們時間太少、人也太少。

gept.pattern: Demonstratives this / that / these / those (指示詞)
gept.meaning_zh: this/these 指近、that/those 指遠,單複數須與名詞一致;those 可後接片語替代名詞。
gept.structure: this/that + 單數N; these/those + 複數N

examples.en: This book is mine; those over there are yours.
examples.zh: 這本書是我的;那邊那些是你的。

examples.en: These shoes are too small.
examples.zh: 這雙鞋太小了。

examples.en: The weather here is better than that in the north.
examples.zh: 這裡的天氣比北部的好。

gept.pattern: another / other / others / the other (其他的區別)
gept.meaning_zh: another + 單數(另一個);other + 複數;others 代替複數名詞;the other(s) 指剩下的特定者。
gept.structure: another + 單數N; other + 複數N; (the) others = (the) other + 複數N

examples.en: Would you like another cup of tea?
examples.zh: 你要再來一杯茶嗎?

examples.en: Some like summer; others prefer winter.
examples.zh: 有些人喜歡夏天,有些人偏好冬天。

examples.en: One twin is tall; the other is short.
examples.zh: 雙胞胎中一個高,另一個矮。

gept.pattern: It-cleft for time / place (it 分裂句強調時間或地點)
gept.meaning_zh: It is/was + 被強調部分 + that/when/where + 其餘,可強調時間或地點狀語。
gept.structure: It is/was + 時間/地點 + that/when/where + S + V

examples.en: It was in Paris that they first met.
examples.zh: 他們是在巴黎初次相遇的。

examples.en: It was last year that I changed jobs.
examples.zh: 我是去年換工作的。

examples.en: It was here that the accident happened.
examples.zh: 意外就是在這裡發生的。

gept.pattern: What S V is / are ... (What 引導的分裂句強調)
gept.meaning_zh: 以 What 引導的名詞子句作主詞,強調動作的內容或結果。
gept.structure: What + S + V + is/are + 被強調部分

examples.en: What I need is a good rest.
examples.zh: 我需要的是好好休息。

examples.en: What surprised me was his honesty.
examples.zh: 讓我驚訝的是他的誠實。

examples.en: What she wants is your support.
examples.zh: 她要的是你的支持。

gept.pattern: the way in which / how (方式子句)
gept.meaning_zh: 表「…的方式」用 the way、the way that 或 the way in which;the way 與 how 不可同時並用。
gept.structure: the way (that / in which) + S + V

examples.en: I like the way you talk.
examples.zh: 我喜歡你說話的樣子。

examples.en: That's the way it works.
examples.zh: 事情就是這樣運作的。

examples.en: She explained the way to solve the problem.
examples.zh: 她說明了解決問題的方法。

gept.pattern: the reason why / for which (原因子句)
gept.meaning_zh: 表「…的原因」用 the reason why、the reason that 或 the reason for which。
gept.structure: the reason why / that / for which + S + V

examples.en: That's the reason why I left.
examples.zh: 那就是我離開的原因。

examples.en: Do you know the reason why she's upset?
examples.zh: 你知道她為何難過嗎?

examples.en: The reason that he failed is laziness.
examples.zh: 他失敗的原因是懶惰。

gept.pattern: such as / like (舉例用法)
gept.meaning_zh: such as、like 用於舉例,表「例如」,後接名詞或名詞片語。
gept.structure: N + such as / like + 例子(名詞)

examples.en: I enjoy sports such as tennis and swimming.
examples.zh: 我喜歡網球和游泳這類運動。

examples.en: Bring something to drink, like water or juice.
examples.zh: 帶點喝的,像水或果汁。

examples.en: Big cities such as Tokyo never sleep.
examples.zh: 像東京這樣的大城市永不沉睡。

gept.pattern: as well as (除…之外也,主詞動詞一致)
gept.meaning_zh: as well as 連接兩者,意為「以及、也」;主詞動詞依 as well as 前的主詞決定。
gept.structure: A + as well as + B + V (動詞依 A 決定)

examples.en: The teacher, as well as the students, was excited.
examples.zh: 老師和學生們都很興奮。

examples.en: He plays the guitar as well as the piano.
examples.zh: 他會彈吉他也會彈鋼琴。

examples.en: She speaks French as well as English.
examples.zh: 她會說法文也會說英文。

gept.pattern: There + appear / seem / exist (there 句型延伸)
gept.meaning_zh: there 句型除 be 動詞外,也可接 seem/appear/exist/remain 等,表存在。
gept.structure: There + seem/appear/exist/remain + to be / 名詞

examples.en: There seems to be a mistake.
examples.zh: 似乎有個錯誤。

examples.en: There appear to be no easy answers.
examples.zh: 看來沒有簡單的答案。

examples.en: There remains much to be done.
examples.zh: 還有很多事要做。

gept.pattern: so far / so good (程度與範圍副詞片語)
gept.meaning_zh: so far 表「到目前為止」,常搭配完成式;區別於 so...that 的結果用法。
gept.structure: S + 完成式 + so far (= until now)

examples.en: So far, everything has gone well.
examples.zh: 到目前為止一切順利。

examples.en: I haven't heard from her so far.
examples.zh: 我到現在還沒她的消息。

examples.en: We've sold ten tickets so far.
examples.zh: 我們目前賣出了十張票。

gept.pattern: enough as determiner (enough 當限定詞)
gept.meaning_zh: enough 修飾名詞時置於名詞前;修飾形容詞/副詞時置於其後(與 too...to 對照)。
gept.structure: enough + N; adj/adv + enough

examples.en: We have enough chairs for everyone.
examples.zh: 我們有足夠的椅子給大家坐。

examples.en: He isn't old enough to drive.
examples.zh: 他還沒到開車的年齡。

examples.en: There isn't enough light in this room.
examples.zh: 這房間光線不夠。

gept.pattern: few / little vs a few / a little (差別,否定 vs 肯定)
gept.meaning_zh: few/little 帶否定意味(幾乎沒有),a few/a little 帶肯定意味(有一些);few/a few 接可數,little/a little 接不可數。
gept.structure: few / a few + 可數複數N; little / a little + 不可數N

examples.en: I have a few questions.
examples.zh: 我有幾個問題。

examples.en: Few people understood the lecture.
examples.zh: 幾乎沒人聽懂那場演講。

examples.en: There's little hope left.
examples.zh: 幾乎沒剩下什麼希望了。

gept.pattern: so that 與 such that 的位置差異 (對照)
gept.meaning_zh: so 接形容詞/副詞、such 接名詞片語;結果子句皆用 + that;區別在 so 後無名詞、such 後有名詞。
gept.structure: so + adj/adv + (a N) + that...; such + (a/an) adj + N + that...

examples.en: It was so good a film that I saw it twice.
examples.zh: 那部片好到我看了兩遍。

examples.en: It was such a good film that I saw it twice.
examples.zh: 那是部好片,好到我看了兩遍。

examples.en: She spoke so softly that I couldn't hear.
examples.zh: 她說得太小聲,我聽不見。

gept.pattern: the + 比較級 of the two (兩者中較…的)
gept.meaning_zh: 比較兩者時,「較…的那一個」用 the + 比較級 + of the two。
gept.structure: the + 比較級 + of the two

examples.en: She is the taller of the two sisters.
examples.zh: 她是兩姊妹中較高的。

examples.en: This is the better of the two plans.
examples.zh: 這是兩個方案中較好的。

examples.en: He is the more talented of the twins.
examples.zh: 他是雙胞胎中較有天分的。

gept.pattern: as ... as possible / as ... as one can (盡可能…)
gept.meaning_zh: as + 形容詞/副詞 + as possible 或 as...as + 主詞 + can,表「盡可能地…」。
gept.structure: as + adj/adv + as possible; as + adj/adv + as + S + can

examples.en: Please reply as soon as possible.
examples.zh: 請盡快回覆。

examples.en: Run as fast as you can.
examples.zh: 盡你所能地快跑。

examples.en: Make it as simple as possible.
examples.zh: 盡量把它弄簡單。

gept.pattern: Possessive of / 's (無生命所有格用法區別)
gept.meaning_zh: 有生命名詞多用 's;無生命名詞多用 of 片語表所有關係。
gept.structure: 有生命N + 's + N; the + N + of + 無生命N

examples.en: This is Mary's bag.
examples.zh: 這是瑪麗的包包。

examples.en: The roof of the house is leaking.
examples.zh: 房子的屋頂在漏水。

examples.en: The title of the book is long.
examples.zh: 這本書的書名很長。

gept.pattern: Verb + that clause omitting should (建議要求動詞接原形)
gept.meaning_zh: suggest/insist/demand/recommend 等後接 that 子句,動詞用原形(should 省略)。
gept.structure: suggest/insist/demand + that + S + (should) + V(原形)

examples.en: The doctor suggested that he rest for a week.
examples.zh: 醫生建議他休息一週。

examples.en: I insist that she be present.
examples.zh: 我堅持她要出席。

examples.en: They demanded that the rule be changed.
examples.zh: 他們要求修改規定。

gept.pattern: It is + adj + that + S + should V (重要必要句型接原形)
gept.meaning_zh: It is important/necessary/essential that 後的子句動詞用原形(should 可省略)。
gept.structure: It is important/necessary/essential + that + S + (should) + V(原形)

examples.en: It is important that everyone be on time.
examples.zh: 每個人準時很重要。

examples.en: It is necessary that he sign the form.
examples.zh: 他必須在表格上簽名。

examples.en: It is essential that we keep this secret.
examples.zh: 我們務必保守這個秘密。

gept.pattern: too ... to V vs so ... that (過於…無法,互換)
gept.meaning_zh: too + 形容詞/副詞 + to V 表「太…以致於不能」,可與 so...that...not 互換。
gept.structure: too + adj/adv + (for sb) + to V (= so + adj/adv + that + S + can't)

examples.en: He is too young to drive.
examples.zh: 他太年輕還不能開車。

examples.en: The box is too heavy for me to lift.
examples.zh: 這箱子太重,我搬不動。

examples.en: It was too dark to see anything.
examples.zh: 太暗了,什麼都看不見。

gept.pattern: in spite of / despite + N (儘管,接名詞)
gept.meaning_zh: in spite of、despite 後接名詞或動名詞,表讓步「儘管」,不可直接接子句。
gept.structure: in spite of / despite + N / V-ing, S + V

examples.en: We went out despite the rain.
examples.zh: 儘管下雨我們還是出門了。

examples.en: In spite of being tired, she kept working.
examples.zh: 儘管很累,她仍持續工作。

examples.en: He smiled despite his pain.
examples.zh: 儘管疼痛,他仍面帶微笑。

gept.pattern: due to / because of vs because (介系詞 vs 連接詞)
gept.meaning_zh: due to、because of 後接名詞片語;because 後接完整子句,兩者不可混用。
gept.structure: due to / because of + N; because + S + V

examples.en: The game was canceled because of the storm.
examples.zh: 比賽因暴風雨取消。

examples.en: He was late because he missed the bus.
examples.zh: 他遲到是因為錯過了公車。

examples.en: Sales dropped due to the bad weather.
examples.zh: 銷售因天氣不佳而下滑。

gept.pattern: on / upon + V-ing (一…就…)
gept.meaning_zh: on / upon + 動名詞,表「一…就…」,等於 as soon as 引導的時間子句。
gept.structure: On / Upon + V-ing, S + V

examples.en: On hearing the news, she screamed.
examples.zh: 一聽到消息,她就尖叫了。

examples.en: Upon arriving home, he fell asleep.
examples.zh: 一到家他就睡著了。

examples.en: On seeing me, the dog ran over.
examples.zh: 一看到我,那隻狗就跑了過來。

gept.pattern: the more ... the more (越…越…)
gept.meaning_zh: the + 比較級..., the + 比較級...,表「越…就越…」的相關變化。
gept.structure: The + 比較級 + S + V, the + 比較級 + S + V

examples.en: The more you practice, the better you get.
examples.zh: 你越練習就越進步。

examples.en: The harder he tried, the worse it became.
examples.zh: 他越努力,情況越糟。

examples.en: The older I get, the more I understand.
examples.zh: 我年紀越大,懂得越多。

gept.pattern: so / such 置句首倒裝 (強調倒裝)
gept.meaning_zh: 以 So + 形容詞/副詞或 Such 置句首加強語氣時,主句須部分倒裝。
gept.structure: So + adj/adv + 助動詞/be + S + that...; Such + be + S + that...

examples.en: So beautiful was the view that we stopped to take photos.
examples.zh: 景色如此美,我們停下來拍照。

examples.en: Such was his fame that everyone knew him.
examples.zh: 他名氣之大,人人都認識他。

examples.en: So loud was the music that we couldn't talk.
examples.zh: 音樂大聲到我們無法交談。

gept.pattern: the same ... as / such ... as (相同與這類的比較)
gept.meaning_zh: the same + 名詞 + as 表「和…一樣」;such + 名詞 + as 表「像…這樣的」。
gept.structure: the same + N + as + 比較對象; such + N + as + 比較對象

examples.en: She has the same bag as me.
examples.zh: 她有跟我一樣的包包。

examples.en: I've never seen such kindness as hers.
examples.zh: 我從沒見過像她這樣的善心。

examples.en: He wore the same shirt as yesterday.
examples.zh: 他穿了跟昨天一樣的襯衫。

gept.pattern: V + O + to be / as (認定句型)
gept.meaning_zh: consider/find/think/regard 等接受詞後,可接 to be 或 as 引介受詞補語。
gept.structure: consider/find + O + (to be) + 補語; regard/see + O + as + 補語

examples.en: We consider him (to be) a genius.
examples.zh: 我們認為他是個天才。

examples.en: I find this book very useful.
examples.zh: 我覺得這本書很有用。

examples.en: They regard her as a leader.
examples.zh: 他們把她視為領導者。
